title: Adelphacme minima
intro: Adelphacme minima, also known as Adelphacme, is a type of creeping fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. Traditionally, Adelphacme minima has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Adelphacme minima include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Adelphacme minima include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe and calm the body.
